meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no testing knowledge (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no testing knowledge. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Testing Knowledge
content.seo_description: Master SaaS pricing and overcome testing gaps as a freelancer. An advanced case study using Antigravity and Claude Code to deliver high-value, tested builds.
content.answer_block: Eliminate the fear of testing by automating your SaaS pricing logic and unit tests simultaneously. By leveraging Claude Code for iterative validation and Antigravity for structural scaffolding, you can deploy robust pricing models that handle edge cases without manual QA, allowing you to ship faster and charge premium rates.
content.intro:
You’ve landed the client, the scope is defined, and the revenue model is set. But then the reality hits: you have zero testing knowledge, and the pricing logic for this SaaS is complex enough to sink the project if a single edge case fails. The deadline is looming, and the pressure to ship without bugs is mounting.

Stop sweating the test suite. By integrating AI-driven scaffolding and autonomous code validation, you can turn a lack of testing knowledge into a competitive advantage. We are shifting from manual trial-and-error to systematic, AI-verified pricing architecture that guarantees reliability while you focus on high-level feature development.

steps.title: Define Pricing Logic Scaffolding
steps.description: Establish the core pricing model architecture using Antigravity to ensure the data structure is immutable and ready for integration.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust TypeScript pricing engine structure that handles tiered subscriptions, prorated billing, and coupon logic. Include interfaces for Price, Subscription, and Discount entities.

steps.title: Automate Unit Test Generation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to interpret your pricing logic and generate comprehensive test suites based on the business requirements.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the pricing engine logic and generate Jest test cases covering edge cases like leap years, mid-month upgrades, and zero-value coupons.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'generate unit tests for all functions in ./src/logic/pricing.ts using Jest'

steps.title: Implement Defensive Validation
steps.description: Introduce Zod schemas to enforce runtime type safety, preventing invalid pricing calculations from reaching the database.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create Zod validation schemas for all subscription input objects to ensure type safety before any calculations are performed.

steps.title: Simulate Billing Edge Cases
steps.description: Use AI to stress-test your pricing logic against extreme scenarios, such as subscription cancellations during a billing cycle.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Simulate a scenario where a user upgrades from a monthly to an annual plan mid-billing cycle. Calculate the exact prorated refund amount.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'stress test prorated calculations with mock user data'

steps.title: Final Integration and Smoke Testing
steps.description: Run an end-to-end smoke test to ensure the pricing engine communicates correctly with the mock payment gateway.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Connect the pricing engine to a mock Stripe API interface and verify the checkout flow returns correct totals.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'execute full pricing integration smoke test'

pro_tips: Treat your pricing engine as a pure function: keep all business logic separate from database side-effects to make testing trivial.
Use Claude Code to generate documentation comments for every pricing rule; this acts as your 'testing spec' for future maintenance.
Always implement a 'dry-run' mode in your pricing service that allows you to log inputs and outputs without hitting the payment gateway.
Leverage Antigravity's 'diff' feature to visualize how small changes in your pricing tiers impact the entire test suite coverage.
If a test fails, don't fix it manually; feed the stack trace back into Claude Code to identify the logic drift immediately.
content.next_steps: Once the pricing engine is live, integrate a monitoring tool like Sentry to track any runtime exceptions, then move on to implementing automated user-onboarding flows using the same AI-driven testing methodology.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting technical debt dictate your freelance career. With the right AI-first stack, you can ship enterprise-grade SaaS pricing that works perfectly on day one. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
